questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop and wait until the light turns green
Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to stop completely and remain stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a flashing red traffic light?
choices: Stop completely, then proceed when it is safe
Slow down but do not stop
Treat it as a green light
Stop only if a vehicle is approaching
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: The signal is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The intersection is closed
Pedestrians have the right-of-way only
questions.explanation: A yellow light warns that red is coming, and you should stop when it is safe rather than racing through.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely before proceeding
The same as a red light
Make a U-turn
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light tells you to slow down and proceed carefully through the intersection.

questions.prompt: An octagonal (eight-sided) red sign always means:
choices: Stop
Yield
Do not enter
Speed limit
questions.explanation: The eight-sided red sign is used only for STOP, requiring a complete stop.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of a two-way road means:
choices: You may not pass
You may pass when clear
The road is one-way
Parking is allowed
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means passing is prohibited.

questions.prompt: A red traffic signal with a green arrow pointing right means:
choices: You may turn right in the direction of the arrow when clear
You must stop and not turn
All traffic may proceed straight
The signal is malfunctioning
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected right to move in the direction shown, even when the main light is red.

questions.prompt: What is the meaning of a triangular sign pointing downward?
choices: Yield the right-of-way
Stop completely
No passing zone
Road work ahead
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, meaning slow down and give the right-of-way to others.

questions.prompt: A white rectangular sign showing a number, such as 50, indicates:
choices: The maximum speed limit
The minimum following distance
A route number
The distance to the next town
questions.explanation: White rectangular regulatory signs with a number show the posted maximum speed limit.

questions.prompt: What color and shape are most warning signs that alert you to hazards ahead?
choices: Yellow diamond
Red octagon
Green rectangle
Blue square
questions.explanation: Diamond-shaped yellow signs warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who has the right-of-way when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
choices: The driver on the right
The driver on the left
The faster vehicle
The larger vehicle
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield to oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
Turn before oncoming traffic regardless of distance
Always go before vehicles turning right
Never yield because you signaled first
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles that are too close to allow a safe turn.

questions.prompt: Before making a right turn on a red light in Vermont, you must:
choices: Come to a complete stop and yield to pedestrians and cross traffic
Slow down but keep rolling through
Turn immediately without stopping
Wait for the light to turn green first
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed only after a full stop and after yielding, unless a sign prohibits it.

questions.prompt: When a pedestrian is in a crosswalk at an intersection, you must:
choices: Yield and let the pedestrian cross
Honk so they move faster
Proceed if you have a green light
Drive around them quickly
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield the right-of-way to pedestrians in a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren, you should:
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop until it passes
Speed up to get out of the area
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must yield to:
choices: Traffic already circulating in the roundabout
Traffic waiting to enter behind you
Pedestrians only after you exit
No one, you always have priority
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering a roundabout must yield to traffic already going around it.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where two cars arrive about the same time, you should yield to:
choices: The vehicle on your right
The vehicle on your left
The vehicle going straight only
Whichever car is newer
questions.explanation: With no signs or signals, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are about to turn or change lanes, you should:
choices: Signal before you move so others know your intentions
Signal only if other cars are nearby
Move first, then signal
Never signal on city streets
questions.explanation: Signaling ahead of a turn or lane change warns other road users of your plan.

questions.prompt: A vehicle already in the intersection when the light changes should be allowed to:
choices: Clear the intersection before you proceed
Be cut off so you can go
Stop in the middle of the intersection
Reverse out of the way
questions.explanation: You must yield to vehicles already in the intersection so they can clear it safely.

questions.prompt: When making a U-turn, you should:
choices: Make it only where it is legal and you can see traffic clearly
Make it anywhere traffic is light
Make it on a curve to save time
Make it across a solid double yellow line freely
questions.explanation: U-turns are permitted only where legal and where you have a clear view of approaching traffic.

questions.prompt: What is the statutory default maximum speed limit in Vermont where no other limit is posted?
choices: 50 mph
30 mph
65 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: Vermont's default statutory maximum speed limit is 50 mph unless another limit is posted.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit on Vermont interstate highways?
choices: 65 mph
55 mph
70 mph
60 mph
questions.explanation: The maximum speed limit on Vermont's interstate highways is 65 mph.

questions.prompt: The three-second rule is used to:
choices: Maintain a safe following distance behind the vehicle ahead
Time how long a yellow light lasts
Decide when to change lanes
Measure your reaction to a horn
questions.explanation: The three-second rule helps you keep enough space to stop safely behind the car in front.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or other poor conditions, you should:
choices: Increase your following distance and slow down
Keep the same speed as in dry weather
Follow more closely to see better
Use cruise control on ice
questions.explanation: Poor conditions require more space and lower speeds because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: A posted speed limit represents:
choices: The maximum speed allowed under ideal conditions
The speed you must always drive
The minimum legal speed
A suggestion you can exceed in good weather
questions.explanation: Posted limits are maximums for good conditions; you must slow down when conditions worsen.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Can block the flow of traffic and cause crashes
Always saves fuel safely
Gives you the right-of-way
Is never a violation
questions.explanation: Going far below the speed of traffic disrupts flow and can lead to collisions.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, your stopping distance:
choices: Increases
Decreases
Stays the same
Becomes zero
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require greater distance to stop because of longer reaction and braking distances.

questions.prompt: If a driver behind you is tailgating, you should:
choices: Increase your following distance from the car ahead
Brake hard to warn them
Speed up well over the limit
Move into the left lane and slow down
questions.explanation: Leaving extra space ahead gives you room to stop gradually and avoid a chain collision.

questions.prompt: You should reduce your speed when approaching:
choices: Curves, intersections, and areas with limited visibility
Only when police are present
Long straight highways
Empty parking lots only
questions.explanation: Slowing for curves, intersections, and blind spots gives you time to react to hazards.

questions.prompt: In a school zone with children present, you should:
choices: Slow down and obey the posted school zone speed limit
Maintain highway speed
Use your horn continuously
Pass stopped cars quickly
questions.explanation: School zones require reduced speeds to protect children who may be crossing.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower traffic should generally:
choices: Keep to the right and use the left lane only to pass
Stay in the left lane
Drive in the center lane only
Change lanes constantly
questions.explanation: Slower vehicles keep right and the left lane is reserved for passing.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where your view is blocked
The road is straight and clear
A broken yellow line is on your side
Traffic ahead is moving slowly on a clear road
questions.explanation: Passing where you cannot see oncoming traffic, such as on hills and curves, is dangerous and illegal.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
Only check your rearview mirror
Change lanes quickly without signaling
Honk and move over
questions.explanation: Checking mirrors and blind spots and signaling ensures the lane is clear before you move.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should not return to your lane until:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
You are even with its front bumper
You have sped well above the limit
The other driver waves you over
questions.explanation: Returning only when you can see the vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow line on your side of the road means:
choices: You may pass when the way is clear
You may never pass
The road is one-way
You must stop
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line on your side permits passing when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: When being passed by another vehicle, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed and stay in your lane
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move into the other lane
Brake suddenly
questions.explanation: Keeping a steady speed and lane position lets the other driver pass safely.

questions.prompt: To complete a pass legally, you should:
choices: Stay within the speed limit while passing
Exceed the speed limit to finish quickly
Pass on the shoulder
Pass across a solid double yellow line
questions.explanation: You must not break the speed limit even to complete a pass.

questions.prompt: Passing is prohibited as you approach:
choices: A railroad crossing or intersection
A long open straightaway
A passing lane sign
A rest area exit
questions.explanation: You may not pass at railroad crossings or intersections where conflicts and limited views are likely.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist on the road, you should:
choices: Give plenty of room and pass only when safe
Pass closely to save space
Honk to make them move
Pass even on a curve
questions.explanation: Cyclists are vulnerable, so you must leave ample space and pass only when it is safe.

questions.prompt: Vermont's Move Over Law requires drivers approaching a stopped emergency or tow vehicle with flashing lights to:
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when it is safe
Maintain speed and stay in the lane
Stop in the travel lane
Pass as quickly as possible
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law requires you to slow down and change lanes away from stopped emergency and tow vehicles when safe.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, so you should:
choices: Avoid lingering alongside a truck and never cut in front of one
Drive closely behind to draft
Pass on the right shoulder
Stay in the truck's blind spot
questions.explanation: Trucks cannot see vehicles in their blind spots and need extra room to stop, so stay visible and keep your distance.

questions.prompt: Because motorcycles are smaller and harder to see, you should:
choices: Check carefully before changing lanes or turning
Assume they will always yield
Follow them closely
Share a lane beside them
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are easy to miss, so look carefully before any lane change or turn.

questions.prompt: When you see a slow-moving vehicle emblem (an orange triangle) on a vehicle ahead, it means:
choices: The vehicle travels slowly, such as farm equipment
The vehicle is an emergency vehicle
You must stop behind it
The vehicle is broken down
questions.explanation: The orange triangle marks slow-moving vehicles like farm equipment, so be ready to slow down.

questions.prompt: When approaching a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should:
choices: Stop and yield, as they may be visually impaired
Honk to alert them
Proceed normally
Speed past them
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog indicates a pedestrian who may be blind, and you must yield.

questions.prompt: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and stop arm out, you must:
choices: Stop and remain stopped until the lights stop flashing
Pass slowly on the left
Honk and continue
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: You must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights to protect children getting on and off.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with bicyclists, you should remember that they:
choices: Have the same rights and duties as other drivers
Must always ride on the sidewalk
Are never allowed on rural roads
Do not need to obey traffic signals
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the same legal rights and responsibilities on the road as motor vehicle drivers.

questions.prompt: If you meet a horse and rider on a rural road, you should:
choices: Slow down and pass with plenty of room
Honk to move them along
Rev your engine
Pass closely at full speed
questions.explanation: Horses can be startled easily, so slow down and give them a wide, quiet berth.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for a driver age 21 or older in Vermont?
choices: 0.08 percent
0.05 percent
0.10 percent
0.02 percent
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are legally impaired at a BAC of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Vermont's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 can be penalized for a BAC of:
choices: 0.02 percent or more
0.08 percent or more
0.10 percent or more
0.05 percent or more
questions.explanation: Vermont's zero-tolerance law penalizes drivers under 21 at a BAC of 0.02 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Vermont law regarding hand-held electronic devices while driving states that:
choices: Use of any hand-held portable electronic device is banned for all drivers
Only texting is banned
Phone use is allowed at red lights
Only drivers over 18 are restricted
questions.explanation: Vermont bans all drivers from using hand-held portable electronic devices while driving.

questions.prompt: Drivers under 18 in Vermont may use a portable electronic device:
choices: Not at all, even hands-free
Only for phone calls
Only for texting
Whenever traffic is light
questions.explanation: Drivers under 18 are prohibited from using any portable electronic device, including hands-free.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Alcohol, illegal drugs, and some prescription medications
Only illegal drugs
Only alcohol
Only over-the-counter cold medicine
questions.explanation: Alcohol, illegal drugs, and even some legal medications can impair driving ability.

questions.prompt: The safest way to handle a phone call or text while driving is to:
choices: Wait until you are safely parked
Hold the phone below the window
Type quickly at a red light
Use your knees to steer
questions.explanation: Handling phone tasks only when parked keeps your attention on driving.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving means anything that:
choices: Takes your eyes, hands, or mind away from driving
Only involves texting
Happens only at night
Only affects new drivers
questions.explanation: Distraction is any activity that takes your eyes, hands, or attention away from the task of driving.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid impaired driving after drinking is to:
choices: Arrange a sober ride or designated driver
Drink coffee before driving
Wait fifteen minutes then drive
Roll down the windows
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from your body, so plan a sober ride if you drink.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Improving your reflexes
Sharpening your vision
Having no effect at low amounts
questions.explanation: Even small amounts of alcohol slow reactions and reduce good judgment behind the wheel.

questions.prompt: To reduce distractions before you start driving, you should:
choices: Set up navigation and music before moving
Adjust them while driving
Have a passenger hold your phone to your ear
Read texts at stoplights
questions.explanation: Setting up devices before you drive keeps your focus on the road once you are moving.

questions.prompt: In Vermont, you must turn on your headlights:
choices: From 30 minutes after sunset to 30 minutes before sunrise and when visibility is under 500 feet
Only when it is fully dark
Only on the interstate
Only when other cars have theirs on
questions.explanation: Vermont requires headlights from 30 minutes after sunset to 30 minutes before sunrise and whenever you cannot see 500 feet ahead.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams in fog.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Slam on the brakes hard
Accelerate to regain control
Turn the wheel sharply away from the skid
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas and steering gently where you want to go helps you recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first in cold weather?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Hilltops
Gravel shoulders
Downhill lanes
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other roads because cold air surrounds them on all sides.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning, where tires ride on a film of water, is best avoided by:
choices: Slowing down and keeping tires properly inflated
Speeding up through water
Braking hard in puddles
Using cruise control in the rain
questions.explanation: Lower speeds and good tires keep your tires in contact with the road and prevent hydroplaning.

questions.prompt: When driving on snow or ice, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
Brake suddenly to test traction
Follow closely to stay in tire tracks
Keep your normal dry-road speed
questions.explanation: Snow and ice greatly increase stopping distance, so slow down and leave more room.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in heavy rain that makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Slow down, turn on low beams, and increase following distance
Turn on high beams and speed up
Turn off your lights to reduce glare
Stop suddenly in your lane
questions.explanation: Reducing speed, using low beams, and leaving extra space help you drive safely in heavy rain.

questions.prompt: Before winter weather arrives, you should make sure that:
choices: Your windshield wipers and defroster work properly
Your radio is tuned
Your seat is fully reclined
Your high beams are always on
questions.explanation: Working wipers and a defroster are essential for visibility in rain, snow, and frost.

questions.prompt: When the sun causes glare that makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Slow down and use your sun visor
Speed up to get past it
Close your eyes briefly
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: Slowing down and using your visor or sunglasses helps you cope with bright glare safely.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb when facing downhill keeps the car from rolling into traffic.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Fully to the left
questions.explanation: Turning wheels away from the curb when facing uphill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls back.

questions.prompt: You may not park:
choices: In front of a fire hydrant or in a crosswalk
On any residential street
In a marked parking space
In a parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking is prohibited where it blocks hydrants, crosswalks, and other access points.

questions.prompt: Before opening your door into the street after parking, you should:
choices: Check for approaching traffic and bicyclists
Open it quickly to save time
Rely on your mirror only
Assume the road is clear
questions.explanation: Checking before opening your door prevents striking passing cars or cyclists.

questions.prompt: When you park your vehicle, you should always:
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park or into gear
Leave it in neutral
Leave the engine running
Leave the doors unlocked for others
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and putting the car in park or gear keeps it from rolling.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle breaks down on the road, you should:
choices: Pull off the road if possible and turn on your hazard lights
Stop in the travel lane and wait
Stand in the roadway to flag help
Leave the vehicle in gear and walk away
questions.explanation: Moving off the road and using hazard lights warns others and keeps you safer.

questions.prompt: Some Vermont towns enforce winter parking bans in order to:
choices: Allow snow plows to clear the streets
Increase parking revenue
Reduce speeding
Encourage carpooling
questions.explanation: Winter parking bans keep streets clear so plows can remove snow.

questions.prompt: A curb painted with a no-parking marking or a posted no-parking sign means you:
choices: Must not park there
May park for a short time
May park only at night
May stop but not park
questions.explanation: No-parking signs and markings prohibit parking in that area.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner's permit in Vermont?
choices: 15 years old
16 years old
14 years old
18 years old
questions.explanation: You may apply for a Vermont learner's permit at age 15.

questions.prompt: While driving with a learner's permit in Vermont, you must be accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver age 25 or older, a parent/guardian, or a driver education instructor
Any licensed driver age 18 or older
No one, if it is daytime
Another permit holder
questions.explanation: A permit holder must have a qualified supervising driver, such as a licensed adult 25 or older, seated beside them.

questions.prompt: Before getting a Junior Driver's License, how many hours of supervised practice driving must you complete?
choices: At least 40 hours, including 10 hours at night
At least 20 hours
At least 60 hours
No specific number
questions.explanation: Vermont requires at least 40 hours of supervised practice, including 10 at night, before a junior license.

questions.prompt: How long must you hold a Vermont learner's permit before moving to a Junior Driver's License?
choices: At least one year
At least three months
At least six months
No minimum period
questions.explanation: You must hold your learner's permit for at least one year before getting a junior license.

questions.prompt: During the first three months with a Junior Driver's License in Vermont, you may carry:
choices: No passengers other than your supervising adult or family
Up to three friends
Any passengers
Only one friend
questions.explanation: For the first three months, junior license holders face passenger restrictions limiting who they can carry.

questions.prompt: How many questions must you answer correctly to pass the Vermont written knowledge test?
choices: At least 80 percent of the questions
At least 60 percent
100 percent
At least 50 percent
questions.explanation: You must score at least 80 percent to pass the Vermont knowledge test.

questions.prompt: To get a learner's permit in Vermont, you must first:
choices: Pass a vision screening and the written knowledge test
Complete 40 hours of driving
Hold a junior license
Take only a road test
questions.explanation: A learner's permit requires passing a vision screening and the written knowledge exam.

questions.prompt: Vermont's program for gradually giving new drivers more privileges is called the:
choices: Graduated Driver License (GDL) program
Provisional master license
Senior driver program
Open road program
questions.explanation: Vermont uses a Graduated Driver License program to phase in driving privileges for new drivers.

questions.prompt: In Vermont, seat belt use is required for:
choices: All drivers and passengers under 18
Only the driver
Only front-seat occupants over 18
No one, it is optional
questions.explanation: Vermont law requires every driver and passenger under 18 to wear a seat belt, and buckling up is the safest choice for all.

questions.prompt: In Vermont, a child under 8 years old must be:
choices: Properly secured in a child restraint or booster seat
Belted in the front seat
Held by an adult
Free to ride unrestrained
questions.explanation: Children under 8 must ride in an appropriate child restraint or booster seat.

questions.prompt: Under Vermont's implied consent law, refusing a breath or blood test results in:
choices: An automatic license suspension
A warning only
No penalty
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: Refusing a chemical test under implied consent leads to automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: A driver convicted of DUI in Vermont can face:
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail time
Only a warning
A small fee with no record
No license consequences
questions.explanation: DUI penalties in Vermont include suspension, fines, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: In Vermont, children under 13 should generally ride:
choices: In the back seat whenever practical
In the front seat
On an adult's lap
Without a restraint
questions.explanation: Vermont recommends children under 13 ride in the back seat whenever practical for safety.

questions.prompt: Repeated serious traffic violations in Vermont can lead to:
choices: License suspension or revocation
A free driving course
Lower insurance rates
No consequences
questions.explanation: Serious or repeated violations can result in suspension or revocation of your license.

questions.prompt: Using a hand-held electronic device while driving in Vermont can result in:
choices: A traffic fine and points
A reward
No penalty for adults
Only a verbal warning every time
questions.explanation: Hand-held device use while driving is a violation that can bring fines and points.

questions.prompt: For a driver under 21, being caught with any measurable alcohol can lead to:
choices: License suspension under the zero-tolerance law
Only a warning
A higher speed limit
No action
questions.explanation: Vermont's zero-tolerance law suspends the licenses of under-21 drivers caught with alcohol.

questions.prompt: Before getting a Junior Driver's License, Vermont also requires you to complete:
choices: A state-approved driver education course
A defensive driving refund course
A motorcycle safety course
Only the road test
questions.explanation: Vermont requires completion of an approved driver education course before a junior license.

questions.prompt: After holding a Junior Driver's License for six months with a clean record, passenger restrictions:
choices: End
Become stricter
Last another year
Apply only at night permanently
questions.explanation: After six months with a clean record, the junior license passenger restrictions are lifted.

questions.prompt: When parallel parking, you should leave your vehicle:
choices: Close to the curb and parallel to it
Angled out into the lane
Two feet from the curb
Facing oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Parallel parking means the car sits close to and parallel with the curb, out of the travel lane.

questions.prompt: You should never stop or park where you would:
choices: Block a travel lane or intersection
Be inside a marked space
Be at a metered spot
Be in a driveway you own
questions.explanation: Stopping or parking where you block traffic or an intersection is unsafe and prohibited.

questions.prompt: When a truck is making a wide right turn, you should:
choices: Stay back and not try to squeeze past on the right
Pass quickly on the right
Honk to hurry it
Drive between the truck and the curb
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn right, so never try to pass them on the right during the turn.

questions.prompt: When you see a bicyclist signaling a turn, you should:
choices: Give them room and let them complete the turn
Pass them immediately
Ignore the signal
Crowd them to the curb
questions.explanation: Respect a cyclist's signal and give them space to turn safely, just as you would another driver.

questions.prompt: Accumulating points from traffic violations in Vermont can lead to:
choices: Higher insurance costs and possible license suspension
Automatic license upgrades
A cash reward
Lower registration fees
questions.explanation: Points from violations can raise insurance costs and, if enough accumulate, suspend your license.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you are required to:
choices: Stop, provide information, and render aid as needed
Leave the scene quickly
Move on if no police are present
Only exchange numbers if you caused it
questions.explanation: Drivers must stop at a crash, exchange information, and help anyone who is injured.

questions.prompt: On a one-way street with multiple lanes, you may:
choices: Use any lane that is open and safe for your route
Only use the far left lane
Only use the far right lane
Drive against the marked direction
questions.explanation: On a one-way street you may use any available lane that suits your intended path.

questions.prompt: If heavy fog makes driving unsafe, the best action is to:
choices: Pull completely off the road and wait until it clears
Speed up to get through it
Stop in your lane
Turn off all lights
questions.explanation: When fog is too dense to drive safely, pull fully off the road and wait for it to lift.
